info.name: APIHUB — AI Chat API
info.description:
Productized AI Chat API for APIHUB.

Auth: admin `api-key` header — set the `apiKey` collection variable.

Collection variables (edit in the Variables tab):
- `baseUrl`  — e.g. `http://localhost:8080`
- `apiKey`   — admin API key
- `chatId`   — auto-filled by «Create Chat» test script; or set manually
- `fileId`   — fill manually from an assistant message that contains a download link
- `fileToken`— fill manually from the download URL in the assistant message

query.description: Keyset cursor — RFC3339 timestamp from the previous page

request.description: Returns the user's chats, newest first (pinned chats float to the top). Keyset-paginated by `lastMessageAt`.
Creates a new chat. The test script auto-saves `chatId` to the collection variable.
Returns metadata for a single chat.
Rename the chat. You can also send `{"pinned": true}` to pin it.
Pin the chat. Max 3 pinned chats per user — server returns 400 APIHUB-AI-4003 if exceeded.
Permanently deletes the chat and all its messages. Returns 204.

query.description: RFC3339 cursor — pass the oldest `createdAt` from previous page

request.description: Returns messages newest first. Paginate with `before=<oldest.createdAt>`. Generated-file links in assistant messages are re-signed on each response.

Synchronous variant — waits for the full LLM response. Intended for scripts and automated tests only; use the streaming endpoint for normal UX.

The `clientMessageId` (`{{$guid}}` generates a fresh UUID per send) enables idempotent retries.

Main UX path — returns `text/event-stream`. Postman renders the raw SSE frames in the response body.

Event sequence (happy path):
  context.compacted   (optional)
  message.assistant.start
  tool.started / tool.completed  (0..N)
  message.assistant.delta * N
  message.assistant.completed
  done

Each frame: `event: <type>\ndata: <JSON>\n\n`
Re-sending the same `clientMessageId` returns the cached assistant message instead of billing another LLM call. Replace PASTE_SAME_UUID_HERE with the UUID used in the original request.

query.description: Short-lived RS256 JWT from the download URL in the assistant message. No session cookie needed.

request.description:
Download a file generated by the assistant (e.g. an IDS Markdown document).

How to get fileId + token:
1. Run a streaming send that triggers IDS generation.
2. Find the `message.assistant.completed` SSE event.
3. In `message.content` look for a markdown link like `[IDS_TCS.md](/api/v1/generated-files/<fileId>?token=<jwt>)`.
4. Copy `<fileId>` → collection variable `fileId`, copy `<jwt>` → collection variable `fileToken`.

Error codes:
  401 APIHUB-AI-3002 — missing / invalid token
  410 APIHUB-AI-4101 — token expired
  404 APIHUB-AI-3002 — file not found or already cleaned up
